heading: Pediatric uses of devices

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A person that submits to the Secretary an application under , or an application (or supplement to an application) or a product development protocol under , shall include in the application or protocol the information described in paragraph (2).section 360j(m) of this titlesection 360e of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a description of any pediatric subpopulations that suffer from the disease or condition that the device is intended to treat, diagnose, or cure; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of affected pediatric patients.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of devices approved in the year preceding the year in which the report is submitted, for which there is a pediatric subpopulation that suffers from the disease or condition that the device is intended to treat, diagnose, or cure;

children.label: B
children.text: any information, based on a review of data available to the Secretary, regarding devices used in pediatric patients but not labeled for such use for which the Secretary determines that approved pediatric labeling could confer a benefit to pediatric patients;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of pediatric devices that receive a humanitarian use exemption under ;section 360j(m) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the number of devices approved in the year preceding the year in which the report is submitted, labeled for use in pediatric patients;

children.label: E
children.text: the number of pediatric devices approved in the year preceding the year in which the report is submitted, exempted from a fee pursuant to ;section 379j(a)(2)(B)(v) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the review time for each device described in subparagraphs (A), (C), (D), and (E);

children.label: G
children.text: the number of devices for which the Secretary relied on data with respect to adults to support a determination of a reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness in pediatric patients; and

children.label: H
children.text: the number of devices for which the Secretary relied on data from one pediatric subpopulation to support a determination of a reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness in another pediatric subpopulation.

children.heading: Determination of pediatric effectiveness based on similar course of disease or condition or similar effect of device on adults
children.label: b Determination of pediatric effectiveness based on similar course of disease or condition or similar effect of device on adults

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the course of the disease or condition and the effects of the device are sufficiently similar in adults and pediatric patients, the Secretary may conclude that adult data may be used to support a determination of a reasonable assurance of effectiveness in pediatric populations, as appropriate.

children.heading: Extrapolation between subpopulations
children.label: 2 Extrapolation between subpopulations
children.text: A study may not be needed in each pediatric subpopulation if data from one subpopulation can be extrapolated to another subpopulation.

children.heading: Pediatric subpopulation
children.label: c Pediatric subpopulation
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “pediatric subpopulation” has the meaning given the term in .section 360j(m)(6)(E)(ii) of this title
